meta_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve No Portfolio (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about a lack of experience. Learn to use Git, Antigravity, and Claude Code to build a high-impact developer portfolio in record time.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is a failure of visibility, not skill. By leveraging AI-assisted Git workflows, you can transform 48 hours of focused coding into a repository of production-ready SaaS modules. Use Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for rapid implementation to ship a professional-grade portfolio today.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a new repository is a freelancer's greatest enemy. You have the technical hunger, but the market demands social proof before they trust you with their capital. You are stuck in the paradox of needing projects to get work, and needing work to get projects.

It is time to break the cycle. We are moving beyond simple coding; we are engineering a 'Proof of Capability' engine. Using Antigravity and Claude Code, you will stop writing boilerplate and start shipping sophisticated, production-grade logic that demonstrates architectural mastery. This is how you outpace the competition and command premium rates.

steps.title: Architect the Core Module
steps.description: Define a high-value micro-SaaS component—like a Stripe-integrated subscription engine—to anchor your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a modular, scalable architecture for a Stripe subscription backend using Node.js and TypeScript. Focus on clean separation of concerns and database schema optimization for PostgreSQL.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --init --prompt 'Generate the directory structure and base schema for a Stripe subscription engine'.

steps.title: Automate Boilerplate Generation
steps.description: Use AI to scaffold the infrastructure, ensuring your code adheres to professional enterprise standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready repository structure including CI/CD configuration, unit testing framework setup, and environment variable validation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Create boilerplate files for authentication, database connection, and API routing'.

steps.title: Implement High-Complexity Logic
steps.description: Focus on the 'meat' of the project—complex state management or algorithm implementation—using Claude Code's deep context awareness.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust webhook handler for Stripe events that accounts for edge cases like subscription failures and downgrades.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --edit 'Implement the webhook controller and ensure all edge cases in the Stripe documentation are covered'.

steps.title: Refactor for Senior-Level Readability
steps.description: Refining your code is where you prove seniority. Use AI to clean up technical debt and enforce strict linting rules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the entire codebase for performance bottlenecks and adherence to SOLID principles. Propose refactoring for better maintainability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --refactor 'Optimize the codebase for readability, apply strict TypeScript types, and remove all magic numbers'.

steps.title: Document and Deploy for Impact
steps.description: A repository without a README is invisible. Generate comprehensive technical documentation to signal professional maturity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional README.md that includes a project overview, setup instructions, architecture diagram (Mermaid), and API documentation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --docs 'Create a comprehensive README.md that explains the technical design decisions and how to deploy this project'.

pro_tips: Always commit in atomic, feature-focused chunks to showcase a clean, logical Git history to potential clients.
Include a 'Design Decisions' section in your README to demonstrate the 'why' behind your code, not just the 'what'.
Use Claude Code to write automated integration tests that run on every push; it proves you prioritize reliability.
Create a 'demo-app' folder inside your repo to show how your core module integrates into a UI, even if the UI is simple.
Tag your releases using Semantic Versioning (SemVer) to show you understand professional software release cycles.
resources: Git Best Practices for Teams: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
content.next_steps: Once your repository is polished, deploy the project to Vercel or AWS and link the live demo in your README. Then, update your LinkedIn and freelance profiles with a direct link to the repository.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to build a portfolio that commands authority. Stop waiting for the 'perfect' project and start creating the evidence of your expertise. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
